cards.back: A page table is a data structure that maps virtual page numbers to physical frame numbers in a computer system.
A single entry in a page table, which contains the virtual page number, a frame number, and other information such as permissions and validity bits.

The level of a page table, which determines the granularity of the mapping between virtual and physical addresses. Common page table levels include page tables, segment tables, and global page tables.
Flags in a page table entry that indicate whether the page table entry is valid and whether the associated frame is free.

An algorithm used to select which frame to replace when a page fault occurs and the system is out of physical memory. Common policies include First-In-First-Out (FIFO), Least Recently Used (LRU), and Least Frequently Used (LFU).
